text:
The was a 18.5 meter (approximately 60-foot) inflatae slider located outside the Nippon Television (NTV) Tower in Shiodome, Tokyo. It was part of the Shiohaku Expo 2012, which ran from 25 July to 26 August 2012. https://eva-fan.com/blog-entry-1687.html 

__TOC__

Event Context

The Shiohaku Expo 2012 was a summer event, organized by NTV, held at Shiodome headquarters. The expo featured a mix of pop culture exhibitions, art installations, and promotional events aimed at engaging the public. These were designed to highlight both NTV's programming and major franchises. As part of the event, the inflatable attraction featuring Rei Ayanami was featured alongside other Evangelion merchandise and events, all promoting the then upcoming release of Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo in November 2012. https://shiodome6.rssing.com/chan-18744648/article2.html The attraction was set up outside the NTV Tower and was designed and created by NTV as well. 

Design and Structure

Measuring at 18.5 meters in length, the tall inflatable slide was modeled after Rei Ayanami wearing her plugsuit. Rei was portrayed lying on her side, a pose that serves both visual impact and structural function. The design appears to make Rei's entire left leg the slide itself. Since the attraction was designed for visitors to slide down, it features a smooth, controlled descent with safety barriers and cushioned surfaces at the end.

Reception

The slide was met with a mix of amusement and confusion. Images circulated on blogs, forums, and social media (namely Reddit and Twitter) with people reacting to the sheer absurdity of the slide. Some fans found the imagery of Rei's left leg being turned into a slide delightfully weird, whereas others found the depiction of the cold, emotionless character as a playground attraction to be a little bizarre. Within the expo itself, the slide drew considerable foot traffic, largely due to its size and peculiarity.
